name: team-composition-analysis
description: This skill should be used when the user asks to "plan team
capabilities: Design team structures for pre-seed through Series A startups
Create role-by-role hiring plans with timeline estimates
Calculate fully-loaded compensation costs including benefits and taxes
Provide equity allocation benchmarks by role and funding stage
Generate organizational reporting structures and span of control guidance
Budget headcount costs aligned with revenue milestones
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Prompt-only skill with no executable code. No security risks detected. The skill provides guidance for team composition, hiring planning, compensation benchmarks, and equity allocation for early-stage startups. No file system access, network operations, external commands, or environment variable access present.
valueStatement: Build the right team at the right time with data-driven hiring plans, compensation benchmarks, and equity allocation strategies. Scale from pre-seed to Series A with role-by-role guidance and budget planning tools.
userTitle: Plan Startup Team Structure and Hiring
limitations: Benchmarks based on US market rates from 2024, may vary by location
Equity ranges are general guidelines, actual allocations depend on company specifics
Hiring timelines are estimates and can vary based on role and market conditions
Does not replace legal or financial advice for equity compensation

useCases.targetUser: First-time startup founders building initial team
useCases.title: Early-Stage Founder Hiring Plan
useCases.description: Founders at pre-seed stage planning their first 5-10 hires with salary and equity allocation

useCases.title: Series A Headcount Budget
useCases.description: VP of Finance planning $2M-$10M ARR stage hiring budget and organizational structure

useCases.targetUser: Founders preparing for seed or Series A fundraising
useCases.title: Equity Pool Design
useCases.description: Founders determining option pool size and equity grants for executive team hires

promptTemplates.title: Basic Team Structure
promptTemplates.prompt: I'm a pre-seed founder with $500K raised. What should my team look like for the next 12 months?
promptTemplates.scenario: Early-stage founder seeking initial hiring guidance

promptTemplates.title: Hiring Plan with Budget
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a hiring plan for a seed-stage SaaS company targeting $1M ARR. Include roles, salaries, equity, and timeline.

promptTemplates.title: Compensation Benchmarking
promptTemplates.prompt: What are the salary and equity ranges for a VP of Engineering at Series A stage in Austin?

promptTemplates.title: Org Chart Design
promptTemplates.prompt: Design the organizational structure for a 30-person startup at Series A with optimal reporting lines.

bestPractices: Multiply base salary by 1.3-1.4 to calculate fully-loaded costs including payroll taxes, benefits, and overhead
Add 2-3 months buffer to hiring timelines to account for recruitment delays and ramp-up time
Reserve 10-15% option pool per funding round (pre-seed through Series A) to ensure adequate equity for hires
antiPatterns: Hiring senior roles too early before validating product-market fit and repeatable sales
Offering equity at the top of ranges without considering stage dilution and pool sustainability
Planning hiring based on idealized timelines without accounting for 6-24 week recruiting cycles

faq.question: What stage does this skill support?
faq.answer: This skill supports startups from pre-seed (0-$500K ARR) through Series A ($2M-$10M ARR), covering team sizes from 2-5 people up to 15-50 people.

faq.question: Are the compensation benchmarks current?
faq.answer: Salary benchmarks are based on 2024 US market rates. Adjust by location: SF/NYC +20-30%, Seattle/Boston/LA +10-20%, Austin/Denver/Chicago +0-10%, remote -10-20%.

faq.question: How do I calculate fully-loaded costs?
faq.answer: Multiply base salary by 1.3-1.4 to account for payroll taxes (7.65%), benefits ($10K-$15K per employee), and overhead ($5K-$10K per employee for workspace, equipment, software).

faq.question: What equity should I offer early employees?
faq.answer: First engineer: 0.5-2.0%. First 5 employees: 0.25-1.0% each. Seed stage VPs: 0.5-1.5%. All equity vests over 4 years with a 1-year cliff.

faq.question: How long does hiring take?
faq.answer: Junior roles: 6-8 weeks. Mid-level: 8-12 weeks. Senior: 12-16 weeks. Executive: 16-24 weeks. Add 2-4 months for productivity ramp-up after hiring.

faq.question: Should I hire full-time or contractors?
faq.answer: Use full-time for core product development, sales, and mission-critical roles. Use contractors for specialized short-term needs (legal, accounting), variable workload (design, marketing), and to test roles before hiring full-time.
